chunks.page_title: 1. Placement Cell Overview — Mission, Objectives & Placement Committee
chunks.section: 1. Placement Cell Overview — Mission, Objectives & Placement Committee
chunks.text: The chunk describes the overall mission and objectives of the MSAJCE Placement Cell and lists the full placement committee with names and positions. In a competitive job market, employers expect graduates to have a range of skills and right experience of the workplace. MSAJCE provides at most care, support and ample opportunities to help students develop requisite skills and expertise and make them employable graduates. The Placement Cell Objectives are to provide Employment Opportunities for students in reputed companies, to organize On and Off Campus Recruitment Drives for students, to Train students on employability skills such as Aptitude Skills, Communication Skills and Technical Skills, to conduct Value Added Certification courses to gain domain specific knowledge, to conduct Mock Aptitude, Mock Interview, Mock GD to prepare students to face the interview with confidence, to arrange In-plant training, Internship and Industrial visits, and to organize workshops on Recent Technologies by industrial experts which benefits students to understand technological innovations. Besides providing support to entrepreneurial ventures, MSAJCE keeps its employability services up to date with the demands of the job market and aims to ensure that graduates are in the strongest position possible when they leave. The following data is from the Placement Committee table: Dr. S. Vijay Ananth is the Placement Director. Mr. L. Tharanikumar is the Assistant Placement Officer. Mr. S.V. Vinodh is a Member. Mr. J.H. Hammer Shield is a Member. Mr. M. Dominic Bro is a Member. Mr. P. Nithin Karthik is a Member.

chunks.page_title: 2. Placement Facilities — Infrastructure for Recruitment Activities
chunks.section: 2. Placement Facilities — Infrastructure for Recruitment Activities
chunks.text: Assistant Placement Officer. Mr. S.V. Vinodh is a Member. Mr. J.H. Hammer Shield is a Member. Mr. M. Dominic Bro is a Member. Mr. P. Nithin Karthik is a Member. This chunk describes the physical and infrastructure facilities available at MSAJCE for conducting placement activities including written tests, group discussions, interviews and career counselling. MSAJCE has well-furnished air-conditioned rooms for written tests, group discussions, and face-to-face interviews, well-established computer labs for online training and examinations, air-conditioned auditorium, seminar halls and meeting halls, and dedicated AC rooms for conducting group discussions. Career counselling is available on campus directing students to choose the best possible stream that meets their requirement in a unique way, and the students have mentors who handle their problems and make them focused about their targets. Year-wise placement details are available for download for 2022–2023, 2021–2022, 2020–2021, 2019–2020, 2018–2019, 2017–2018, and 2016–2017. Internship details are available for download for 2020–2021, 2019–2020, 2018–2019, 2017–2018, and 2016–2017, and the internship report format for 2025 is also available.

chunks.text: In pursuit of MSAJCE's vision and mission to make students deployable and equip them with upcoming emerging industrial demands, various Technology Centres were established in the year 2019-2020 to educate, train and up-skill students in niche technologies. The courses and training are designed based on the recommendations of AICTE and India Skill Report 2020. The Technology Centers, established with state-of-art facilities, provide an ambient learning environment for research and innovation. Each centre is collaborated with industry/government organizations to provide certifications, onsite/offsite projects and internships.

chunks.text: applications considering technical and design aspects of AR and VR, develop PC/Mobile-based VR applications, develop 3D Visualization and Visualization projects, and design applications in Gaming, Architecture, Manufacturing, Retail, Healthcare, etc. 1. Introduction to Virtual Reality (6 Hours): Fundamental Concept and Components of VR – Primary Features and Present Development – Computer graphics – Real time computer graphics – Flight Simulation – Benefits of VR – Historical development – 3D Computer Graphics: Virtual world space, positioning the virtual observer, perspective projection, human vision, stereo perspective projection, 3D clipping, Colour theory, Simple 3D modelling, Illumination models, Reflection models, Shading algorithms, Radiosity, Hidden Surface Removal, Realism-Stereographic image. 2. Interactive Techniques in VR (6 Hours): From 2D to 3D – 3D space curves – 3D boundary representation – Geometric Transformations – Frames of reference – Modeling transformations – Instances – Picking – Flying – Scaling the VE – Collision detection – Generic VR system: Virtual environment, Computer environment, VR technology, Model of interaction, VR Systems. 3. Visual Computation in VR (6 Hours): Animating the Virtual Environment – Dynamics of numbers – Linear and Nonlinear interpolation – Animation of objects – Shape and object inbetweening – Free-form deformation – Particle system – Physical Simulation: Objects in gravitational field, Rotating wheels, Elastic collisions, Projectiles, Simple pendulum, Springs, Flight dynamics. 4. Augmented and Mixed Reality (6 Hours): Taxonomy, technology and features of AR – Difference between AR and VR – Challenges with AR – AR systems and functionality – AR methods – Visualization techniques – Wireless displays in educational AR – Mobile projection interfaces – Marker-less tracking – Enhancing interactivity in AR – Evaluating AR systems. 5. Multiple Models of Input and Output Interface in VR (6 Hours): Human factors – VR Hardware: Head-coupled displays, Acoustic hardware, Integrated VR systems – VR Software: Modeling virtual world, Physical simulation, VR toolkits, Introduction to VRML – Input: Tracker, Sensor, Digital Glove, Movement Capture, Video-based Input, 3D Menus, 3D Scanner – Output: Visual/Auditory/Haptic Devices. 6. Application of VR in Digital Entertainment (6 Hours): VR in Film & TV Production – VR in Physical Exercises and Games – Demonstration of Digital Entertainment by VR.

The objectives are to gain basic theoretical knowledge and practical skills of BIM techniques, interpret 2D & 3D drawings and structural analysis, understand regulations as per National Building Code, and prepare students for actual work situations through practical training. The outcomes are to prepare a component to meet desired needs within realistic constraints (economic, environmental, social, ethical, sustainability), create 2D drawings of building, industrial structures and various framed structures using AUTOCAD software, create and simulate a virtual building model using REVIT ARCHITECTURE software with intelligent building elements, and design and analyse different types of structures (liquid retaining structures, bridge components, retaining wall, industrial and commercial structures) in 3D form using STAAD.Pro software to predict performances before construction. Software Used: AUTOCAD, REVIT ARCHITECTURE, STAAD.Pro

chunks.text: abroad. Students also have the opportunity to intern or work full-time abroad, and can receive a Personalised LinkedIn recommendation, along with a Letter of Recommendation after successful Internship/techno programs completion. The department has a fully Techno-Entrepreneurship Innovation lab with hardware and software tools with licenses for development and research projects. The department also has recognition as one of the top National Academies for the innovation lab. They provide essential curriculum and resource kits for comprehensive training and industry readiness, alongside scalable infrastructure to extend training to corporates and professionals from other industries. The department provides up to 10 years assured training support from MIT Square to empower faculty, research scholars and students and, based on NEP 2020, support is provided in regional languages as well. The government initiatives supported are Digital India, Make in India, Startup India, Standup India, Skill India and Self-reliant India (Atmanirbhar Bharat). The following data is from the MSAJCE Contact Information table: Mohamed Sathak A.J. College of Engineering (MSAJCE) is the institution, located at 34, Rajiv Gandhi Salai (OMR), Siruseri IT Park, Siruseri, Chennai – 603103. The phone number is +91 99400 04500, and the email is msajce.office@gmail.com. The grievance email is grievance@msajce-edu.in and the website is www.msajce-edu.in. The institution is affiliated to Anna University Chennai, approved by AICTE New Delhi, and operates under the aegis of Mohamed Sathak Trust, approved by the Government of Tamil Nadu.

MSAJCE Alumni Association Overview Alumni association of Mohamed Sathak AJ College of Engineering welcomes you all to join us. This association is formed to reconnect and foster intellectual and emotional relationship between alumni and parent organisation. Also to bring in goodwill and voluntary commitment to support future engineers of the institution and provide industry-institute relationship and enthusiastically participate in various activities organised by the college. Our Objectives: 1. To foster a sense of connectedness back to the institution 2. To establish and renew student relationship 3. To involve in various activities of the college 4. To provide assistance to needful students by way of funds and scholarship 5. To gather and maintain database of employment and assist in placements 6. To arrange teaching and training classes to students 7.
